---
title: "Test"
format: pdf
editor: visual
---

# Pre-processing först

(Baseline här = första biomarkörs-datumet (oavsett ptau eller nfl), INTE första diagnosstatus-datumet, som vi har i officiella dokumentet)

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(JMbayes2)
library(lme4) # decide which package will we use for lme
library(nlme) # decide which package will we use for lme
library(survival)
library(ggplot2)
library(glmmTMB)
library(DHARMa) # for running residual diagnostics on GLMM
```

```{r}
# Loading data
#Milou
# NfL data
load("ADNIMERGE2_unpacked/ADNIMERGE2/data/BLENNOWPLASMANFLLONG.rda")
nfl_data <- BLENNOWPLASMANFLLONG
# P-tau181 data
load("ADNIMERGE2_unpacked/ADNIMERGE2/data/UGOTPTAU181.rda")
ptau_data <- UGOTPTAU181
# Diagnosis data
load("ADNIMERGE2_unpacked/ADNIMERGE2/data/DXSUM.rda")
diagnosis_data <- DXSUM
# Demographics data
load("ADNIMERGE2_unpacked/ADNIMERGE2/data/PTDEMOG.rda")
demographics <- PTDEMOG
# Apoe data
load("ADNIMERGE2_unpacked/ADNIMERGE2/data/APOERES.rda")
apoe <- APOERES
```

```{r}
# checking observations with more than one measurement recorded on the same exam date for nfl
nfl_double <- nfl_data %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .by = c(RID, EXAMDATE)) %>%
  filter(n > 1L) %>%
  select(RID, EXAMDATE)

# removing the doubled observations
nfl_data <- nfl_data %>%
  group_by(RID, EXAMDATE) %>%
  filter(n() == 1) %>%
  ungroup()

# checking observations with more than one measurement recorded on the same exam date for ptau
ptau_double <- ptau_data %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .by = c(RID, EXAMDATE)) %>%
  filter(n > 1L)  %>%
  select(RID, EXAMDATE)

# extracting the doubled observations
# ptau_data %>%
#    filter(RID %in% ptau_double$RID) %>% 
#    filter(EXAMDATE %in% ptau_double$EXAMDATE)

# removing the doubled observations
ptau_data <- ptau_data %>%
  group_by(RID, EXAMDATE) %>%
  filter(n() == 1) %>%
  ungroup()
```

```{r}
# We select the required nfl variables
nfl_data <- nfl_data %>%
  select(RID, VISCODE2, EXAMDATE, PLASMA_NFL)

# We select the required ptau variables
ptau_data <- ptau_data %>%
  select(RID, VISCODE2, EXAMDATE, PLASMAPTAU181)

# We select the required diagnosis variables
diagnosis_data <- diagnosis_data %>%
  select(ORIGPROT, COLPROT, RID, VISCODE2, EXAMDATE, DIAGNOSIS)

# We add _dx on the diagnosis variables 
diagnosis_data_dx <- diagnosis_data %>%
  rename_with(~paste0(.x, "_dx"), -c(RID, VISCODE2))

# We merge NFL, ptau and diagnosis data for longitudinal data by RID and VISCODE2
long_data <- nfl_data %>%
  left_join(ptau_data, by = c("RID", "VISCODE2"), suffix = c("_nfl", "_ptau")) %>%
  left_join(diagnosis_data_dx, by = c("RID", "VISCODE2"))

# We merge NFL, ptau and diagnosis data for survival data, only by RID 
surv_data <- nfl_data %>%
  left_join(ptau_data, by = c("RID", "VISCODE2"), suffix = c("_nfl", "_ptau")) %>%
  left_join(diagnosis_data_dx, by = c("RID"))
```

```{r}
# We remove missing values 
long_data <- na.omit(long_data)
surv_data <- na.omit(surv_data)

# We re-count missing values
sapply(long_data, function(x) sum(is.na(x)))
sapply(surv_data, function(x) sum(is.na(x)))
```

```{r}
# We only keep the demographics variables we need and one row per person 
demographics_small <- demographics %>%
  select(RID, PTDOBYY, PTGENDER) %>%
  distinct(RID, .keep_all = TRUE)

# We define APOE4 status based on number of E4 alleles
apoe <- apoe %>%
  mutate(APOE4_status = case_when(
    GENOTYPE == "4/4" ~ "2 alleles",
    GENOTYPE %in% c("2/4", "3/4") ~ "1 allele",
    TRUE ~ "0 alleles"
  ))

# We merge gender with merged_data and surv_data
long_data <- long_data %>%
  left_join(demographics_small, by = "RID")
surv_data <- surv_data %>%
  left_join(demographics_small, by = "RID")

# We merge apoe4 status with merged_data and surv_data
long_data <- merge(long_data, apoe[, c("RID", "APOE4_status")], by="RID")
surv_data <- merge(surv_data, apoe[, c("RID", "APOE4_status")], by="RID")

# We make sure that gender and apoe4 status are factors
long_data$PTGENDER <- as.factor(long_data$PTGENDER)
surv_data$PTGENDER <- as.factor(surv_data$PTGENDER)
long_data$APOE4_status <- factor(long_data$APOE4_status,
                                 levels = c("0 alleles", "1 allele", "2 alleles"))
surv_data$APOE4_status <- factor(surv_data$APOE4_status,
                                 levels = c("0 alleles", "1 allele", "2 alleles"))

# We create age_at_visit 
long_data$age_at_visit <- as.numeric(format(long_data$EXAMDATE_ptau, "%Y")) - long_data$PTDOBYY
surv_data$age_at_visit <- as.numeric(format(surv_data$EXAMDATE_ptau, "%Y")) - surv_data$PTDOBYY
```

```{r}
# We filter individuals with at least 3 measurements of ptau_data
keep = unique((ptau_data %>% group_by(RID) %>% filter(n() > 2))$RID)
long_data <- long_data %>% filter(RID %in% keep)
surv_data <- surv_data %>% filter(RID %in% keep)

# We check number of individuals with at least 3 measurements 
length(keep)
```

```{r}
# checking which observations have the same examdate for nfl and ptau
common = which(long_data$EXAMDATE_nfl %in% long_data$EXAMDATE_ptau)

# checking the observations with different exam dates
long_data[-common, ]

# dates aren't too different, and only one observation at baseline, but to be sure create the baseline date out of the earlier of the two dates
merged_data <- long_data %>%
  group_by(RID) %>%
  mutate(baseline_date = min(EXAMDATE_ptau, EXAMDATE_nfl)) %>%
  ungroup()

# checking which observations have the same examdate for nfl and ptau
common = which(long_data$EXAMDATE_nfl %in% long_data$EXAMDATE_ptau)

# checking the observations with different exam dates (9 obs)
long_data[-common, ]

# dates aren't too different, and only one observation at baseline, but to be sure create the baseline date out of the earlier of the two dates
long_data <- long_data %>%
  group_by(RID) %>%
  mutate(baseline_date = min(EXAMDATE_ptau, EXAMDATE_nfl)) %>%
ungroup()
```

```{r}
# We log transform the biomarkers 
long_data$log_PLASMAPTAU181 <- log(long_data$PLASMAPTAU181)
long_data$log_PLASMA_NFL <- log(long_data$PLASMA_NFL)

surv_data$log_PLASMAPTAU181 <- log(surv_data$PLASMAPTAU181)
surv_data$log_PLASMA_NFL <- log(surv_data$PLASMA_NFL)
```

```{r}
core <- surv_data %>%
  group_by(RID) %>%
  summarise(
    
    # Baseline = first observed contact across all sources
    baseline = min(c(EXAMDATE_ptau, EXAMDATE_nfl), na.rm = TRUE),
    
    # Age at baseline 
    age_at_baseline = age_at_visit[
      EXAMDATE_ptau == baseline |
      EXAMDATE_nfl  == baseline 
    ][1],
    
    # First dementia diagnosis after baseline
    event_date = {
      dx_dates <- EXAMDATE_dx[DIAGNOSIS_dx == "Dementia"]
      dx_dates <- dx_dates[!is.na(dx_dates)]
      dx_dates <- dx_dates[dx_dates > baseline]
      
      if (length(dx_dates) == 0) as.Date(NA) else min(dx_dates)
    },
    
    # Last follow-up across all sources
    last_followup = max(c(EXAMDATE_ptau, EXAMDATE_nfl, EXAMDATE_dx), na.rm = TRUE),
    
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  
  mutate(
    
    # Event indicator
    event = as.integer(!is.na(event_date)),
    
    # Time-to-event
    time_to_event = if_else(
      event == 1,
      as.numeric(event_date - baseline) / 365.25,
      as.numeric(last_followup - baseline) / 365.25
    )
  )

# We keep one row per participant and attach all original variables
surv_data <- surv_data %>%
  distinct(RID, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  left_join(core, by = "RID")

# We tabulate the ptau event indicator (1 = dementia, 0 = censored)
table(surv_data$event)
```

```{r}
long_data <- long_data %>%
  left_join(core %>% select(RID, baseline), by = "RID") %>%
  
  mutate(
    time_years_ptau = as.numeric(EXAMDATE_ptau - baseline) / 365.25,
    time_years_nfl  = as.numeric(EXAMDATE_nfl  - baseline) / 365.25
  )
```

# M3 - Time-dependent Cox PH model

Construction of start–stop time intervals and event indicators

```{r}
td_data <- long_data %>%
  
  # We add survival information to each longitudinal record
  left_join(
    surv_data %>%
      select(RID, event, time_to_event),
    by = "RID"
  ) %>%
  
  # We sort observations within each individual by time
  arrange(RID, time_years_ptau) %>%
  group_by(RID) %>%
  
  # We define start–stop intervals based on visit times
  mutate(
    time_start = lag(time_years_ptau, default = 0),
    time_end   = time_years_ptau
  ) %>%
  
  # We remove invalid (zero-length) intervals
  filter(time_end > time_start) %>%
  
  # We define the event as occurring in the interval that contains the event time
  mutate(
    event_interval = as.integer(
      event == 1 &
      time_to_event > time_start &
      time_to_event <= time_end
    )
  ) %>%
  
  ungroup()

# Checking event interval distribution
table(td_data$event_interval)
```

Fitted time-dependent Cox PH model

```{r}
# Fitted Cox PH model with time-dependent covariates using p-tau181
surv_model_ptau_td <- coxph(
  Surv(time_start, time_end, event_interval) ~ 
    log_PLASMAPTAU181 + age_at_visit + PTGENDER + APOE4_status,
  data = td_data
)

summary(surv_model_ptau_td)
```

```{r}
# Fitted Cox PH model with time-dependent covariates using NfL 
surv_model_nfl_td <- coxph(
  Surv(time_start, time_end, event_interval) ~ 
    log_PLASMA_NFL +
    age_at_visit +
    PTGENDER +
    APOE4_status,
  data = td_data
)

summary(surv_model_nfl_td)
```

```{r}
# Fitted Cox PH model with time-dependent covariates using both p-tau181 and NfL 
surv_model_both_td <- coxph(
  Surv(time_start, time_end, event_interval) ~ 
    log_PLASMAPTAU181 +
    log_PLASMA_NFL +
    age_at_visit +
    PTGENDER +
    APOE4_status,
  data = td_data
)

summary(surv_model_both_td)
```

```{r}
##### 3. For the Cox PH models with time-dependent covariates
# For model with p-tau181
cox.zph(surv_model_ptau_td)

# For model with NfL
cox.zph(surv_model_nfl_td)

# For model with both
cox.zph(surv_model_both_td)
```
